bullets.text: Three Iranian tankers carrying crude oil have successfully passed a US blockade in the Gulf of Oman, according to ship-tracking data.
Israeli nationalists are increasingly disregarding established conventions for sharing the al-Aqsa mosque compound, threatening the status quo at Jerusalem's holiest site.

A seven-year-old boy in Somalia, Abdiqadir Salah, was injured in a US airstrike and may lose his ability to walk without a $750 operation, while the US denies civilian casualties and offers no compensation.
A convicted paedophile has been authorised by the NSW government to manage nearly a dozen apartment buildings across Sydney, including those frequented by young families.
Qantas has announced it will launch its 20-hour non-stop flight from Sydney to London next year.
A bill aimed at restricting late-stage abortions has been defeated in the South Australian lower house.
The Federal Reserve has shifted its stance on interest rates, no longer favoring cuts, as inflation has nearly doubled its target due to the war in Iran.
US retail sales have shown a jump, indicating that consumers are managing the impact of rising petrol prices and the economy remains robust.
Wall Street banks are reviving sales of risky leveraged loans that they previously struggled to offload, capitalizing on increased demand for such debt.
